coverage: 100% of reachable node types across the 43-group watertight Bolt closure.
validation: The authored 13-pass heal branch is manifold in both Blender and GN-VM. Blender's native OpenVDB result varied from 16,526-16,598 vertices across ten sequential runs; the stored 16,570 / 16,572 reference is one valid schedule result. The deterministic browser result is 16,222 / 16,224 (2.10% below that reference) with 100% node coverage. Raycast follows Blender's float32 normalization, watertight triangle test, BLI_kdopbvh construction, and direction-dependent traversal. Re-importing the current pass-one browser mesh into Blender and sampling the same 43 x 43 x 71 SDF lattice matches Blender's inside/outside classification at 131,278 of 131,279 samples (99.9992%). New repeat-isolation probes show that Blender itself emits both 21,636 / 21,810 and 21,642 / 21,818 for the one-pass graph; GN-VM's deterministic 21,642 / 21,818 result exactly matches the latter Blender schedule, with bounds within 9.6e-7. At two passes Blender emitted 15,572 / 15,678 in a serial run and 15,470 / 15,560 in five concurrent runs, versus deterministic GN-VM 15,324 / 15,434. The remaining full-chain gap is therefore a compounded OpenVDB scheduling/determinism boundary, not evidence that the browser's one-pass surface-net table is structurally wrong. Full 13-pass validation takes about 153 seconds.
